extraction.one_line_summary: NAESEN Oliver won final stage sprint after gaining bonus seconds in golden kilometer breakaway, while DE PLUS Laurens secured overall GC by taking 7 bonus seconds mid-stage.
tactical_patterns: De Plus neutralized GC threat by entering breakaway himself with 65km remaining, controlling bonus seconds directly rather than chasing.
Golden kilometer triple-bonus format (20km out) forced GC contenders into breakaway rather than allowing sprinter teams to control.
Van Avermaet/Naesen cooperation in break despite stage ambitions suggests priority was denying Wellens bonus seconds for teammate De Plus GC protection.
Hershey attacked De Plus at intermediate sprints while in same breakaway, unusual aggression revealing Jumbo-Visma internal GC calculation over stage win.

rider_signals.DE PLUS Laurens: positive: secured GC by infiltrating breakaway himself to control bonus seconds, tactically mature for preventing gaps rather than just riding defensively
rider_signals.NAESEN Oliver: positive: won stage from 4-rider break despite working for Van Avermaet's GC interest, suggests exceptional form to overcome tactical compromise
rider_signals.WELLENS Tim: negative: finished 38 seconds down on break containing GC rival, inability to follow or organize chase cost him podium position
rider_signals.HERSHEY Marc: positive: 20-year-old attacked De Plus within breakaway for intermediate bonuses despite same team, shows aggressive GC ambition overriding stage hierarchy
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: neutral: worked in break to deny Wellens bonuses for De Plus GC benefit, settled for second in stage sprint suggesting team orders prioritized GC
